babyDevelopment: Your baby's brain is growing rapidly—about 100,000 new neurons every minute! Tiny hands and feet are developing, looking like little paddles. The nostrils are forming, and the mouth and tongue are beginning to take shape.
bodyChanges: Your uterus has doubled in size, though you likely still don't have a visible 'bump'. Frequent urination increases as the uterus presses on the bladder. Acne or 'pregnancy glow' may appear due to hormonal surges.
tips: Increase your fiber intake to prevent constipation
Discuss any family history of genetic conditions with your provider
Try acupressure bands for persistent nausea

do: Wash all fruits and vegetables thoroughly
Take time for mid-day naps if possible
dont: Use retinol or salicylic acid products without approval

travelAdvisory.note: Travel is fine; prioritize rest and frequent stops for water/restroom.

nutrition: Lean beef or lentils for iron
warningSignsToWatch: Inability to tolerate even small sips of water
milestoneDescription: The foundations of thought and movement are expanding vastly this week.
